name: pytest
context: Write and run Python tests with pytest — fixtures, parametrize, markers, async testing, coverage, parallel execution, and plugin ecosystem
info: pytest is the de facto standard Python testing framework. It uses plain assert statements with detailed introspection on failure — no self.assertEqual boilerplate. The fixture system provides dependency injection with scoped setup/teardown (function, class, module, session), yield fixtures for cleanup, and conftest.py for shared fixtures across directories. Parametrize drives data-driven tests with @pytest.mark.parametrize. The plugin ecosystem extends capabilities: pytest-cov for coverage reporting, pytest-asyncio for async/await test support, pytest-mock for a mocker fixture wrapping unittest.mock, pytest-xdist for parallel execution across CPUs, pytest-randomly for order randomization, and pytest-timeout for hanging test protection. Configuration lives in pyproject.toml under [tool.pytest.ini_options]. Test discovery follows conventions: files named test_*.py or *_test.py, functions prefixed test_, classes prefixed Test. See [[testing/mocking]] for mock strategies, [[testing/patterns]] for structural patterns, and [[testing/ci]] for pipeline integration.
triggers: run tests in parallel with xdist
body:
# pytest — Python Testing Framework

pytest is the standard testing framework for Python. It uses plain `assert` statements, automatic test discovery, powerful fixtures for dependency injection, and a rich plugin ecosystem.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Install pytest and common plugins
pip install pytest pytest-cov pytest-asyncio pytest-mock pytest-xdist

# Run all tests
pytest

# First test file: tests/test_example.py
```

```python
def test_addition():
    assert 1 + 1 == 2

def test_string():
    s = "hello"
    assert s.upper() == "HELLO"
    assert s.startswith("he")
```

No imports needed for basic tests. pytest rewrites `assert` to show detailed failure diffs automatically.

## Writing Tests

```python
import pytest

def test_basic_assertion():
    result = {"status": "ok", "count": 42}
    assert result["status"] == "ok"
    assert result["count"] > 0

def test_exception_raised():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="must be positive"):
        validate(-1)

def test_approximate_float():
    assert 0.1 + 0.2 == pytest.approx(0.3)

def test_collection_contents():
    items = get_items()
    assert len(items) == 3
    assert "expected" in items
```

Key principle: use plain `assert` for everything. pytest introspects the expression on failure and shows exactly what went wrong — which side differed, the actual values, even dict diffs.

## Test Discovery

pytest discovers tests by convention:

| Convention | Pattern |
|------------|---------|
| Files | `test_*.py` or `*_test.py` |
| Functions | prefixed with `test_` |
| Classes | prefixed with `Test` (no `__init__`) |
| Methods | prefixed with `test_` inside `Test` classes |
| Directories | configure with `testpaths` in config |

```bash
# Collect and show tests without running
pytest --collect-only
pytest --co  # shorthand

# Run specific file
pytest tests/test_api.py

# Run specific test by name
pytest tests/test_api.py::test_login

# Run tests matching a pattern
pytest -k "login or logout"
pytest -k "not slow"
```

## Fixtures

Fixtures provide reusable setup, teardown, and dependency injection. Declare them with `@pytest.fixture` and request them by name as test parameters.

### Basic Fixtures

```python
import pytest

@pytest.fixture
def sample_user():
    return {"id": 1, "name": "Alice", "email": "alice@example.com"}

@pytest.fixture
def api_client():
    return TestClient(app)

def test_user_name(sample_user):
    assert sample_user["name"] == "Alice"

def test_create_user(api_client, sample_user):
    resp = api_client.post("/users", json=sample_user)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
```

### Yield Fixtures (Setup + Teardown)

Code after `yield` runs as teardown — guaranteed even if the test fails.

```python
@pytest.fixture
def db_session():
    session = SessionLocal()
    yield session
    session.rollback()
    session.close()

@pytest.fixture
def temp_config(tmp_path):
    config_file = tmp_path / "config.toml"
    config_file.write_text('[app]\nname = "test"')
    yield config_file
    # tmp_path is cleaned up automatically
```

### Fixture Scopes

| Scope | Lifetime | Use Case |
|-------|----------|----------|
| `function` | Per test (default) | Mutable state, isolated setup |
| `class` | Per test class | Shared across methods in a class |
| `module` | Per test file | Expensive setup shared in a file |
| `package` | Per test package | Shared across a test directory |
| `session` | Entire test run | DB connections, server startup |

```python
@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def database():
    engine = create_engine("sqlite:///:memory:")
    Base.metadata.create_all(engine)
    yield engine
    engine.dispose()

@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def expensive_resource():
    resource = load_large_dataset()
    yield resource
```

### Fixture Factories

Return a callable from a fixture to create multiple instances with different parameters.

```python
@pytest.fixture
def make_user():
    created = []
    def _make_user(name="Alice", role="user"):
        user = User(name=name, role=role)
        created.append(user)
        return user
    yield _make_user
    for user in created:
        user.delete()

def test_admin(make_user):
    admin = make_user(name="Bob", role="admin")
    assert admin.can_access_admin_panel()
```

### Parametrized Fixtures

```python
@pytest.fixture(params=["sqlite", "postgresql", "mysql"])
def db_engine(request):
    engine = create_engine(get_url(request.param))
    yield engine
    engine.dispose()

def test_query(db_engine):
    # runs 3 times, once per database backend
    result = db_engine.execute("SELECT 1")
    assert result is not None
```

### Built-in Fixtures

```python
def test_temp_files(tmp_path):
    f = tmp_path / "data.json"
    f.write_text('{"key": "value"}')
    assert f.exists()

def test_env_var(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setenv("API_KEY", "test-key-123")
    monkeypatch.delenv("SECRET", raising=False)
    assert os.environ["API_KEY"] == "test-key-123"

def test_stdout(capsys):
    print("hello world")
    captured = capsys.readouterr()
    assert captured.out == "hello world\n"

def test_logging(caplog):
    import logging
    with caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING):
        logging.warning("disk almost full")
    assert "disk almost full" in caplog.text
```

### conftest.py

Shared fixtures live in `conftest.py`. pytest loads them automatically — no imports needed.

```python
# tests/conftest.py
import pytest

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def reset_env(monkeypatch):
    """Reset environment for every test."""
    monkeypatch.setenv("ENV", "test")
    monkeypatch.delenv("API_KEY", raising=False)

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def app():
    return create_app(testing=True)

@pytest.fixture
def auth_headers():
    return {"Authorization": "Bearer test-token-abc"}
```

Conftest files cascade: `tests/conftest.py` is available to all tests; `tests/api/conftest.py` is available only under `tests/api/`. You can have one conftest per directory.

## Parametrize

Run the same test logic with multiple sets of inputs.

```python
@pytest.mark.parametrize("input,expected", [
    ("hello", "HELLO"),
    ("world", "WORLD"),
    ("", ""),
    ("123", "123"),
])
def test_uppercase(input, expected):
    assert input.upper() == expected

# With IDs for readable output
@pytest.mark.parametrize("status_code,expected_ok", [
    (200, True),
    (201, True),
    (404, False),
    (500, False),
], ids=["ok", "created", "not-found", "server-error"])
def test_response_ok(status_code, expected_ok):
    assert is_success(status_code) == expected_ok

# Cross-product: multiple decorators
@pytest.mark.parametrize("x", [1, 2, 3])
@pytest.mark.parametrize("y", [10, 20])
def test_multiply(x, y):
    assert x * y > 0  # runs 6 times

# Parametrize with marks
@pytest.mark.parametrize("value,expected", [
    (1, 1),
    (0, 0),
    pytest.param(-1, 1, marks=pytest.mark.xfail(reason="abs not implemented")),
])
def test_absolute(value, expected):
    assert my_abs(value) == expected
```

## Markers

Markers label tests for selective running and special behavior.

### Built-in Markers

```python
@pytest.mark.skip(reason="Not implemented yet")
def test_future_feature():
    ...

@pytest.mark.skipif(
    sys.platform == "win32",
    reason="Unix-only functionality"
)
def test_unix_permissions():
    ...

@pytest.mark.xfail(reason="Known bug #456")
def test_known_issue():
    assert buggy_function() == "correct"  # expected to fail

@pytest.mark.xfail(strict=True)
def test_must_fail():
    assert False  # strict=True means passing is a failure
```

### Custom Markers

Register custom markers in config, then use them to group tests.

```python
@pytest.mark.slow
def test_large_dataset():
    ...

@pytest.mark.integration
def test_database_connection():
    ...
```

```bash
pytest -m "not slow"            # skip slow tests
pytest -m "integration"          # only integration tests
pytest -m "not integration and not slow"  # unit tests only
```

## Coverage

Use pytest-cov for coverage reporting.

```bash
# Terminal report with missing lines
pytest --cov=src --cov-report=term-missing

# HTML report
pytest --cov=src --cov-report=html

# XML report for CI (Codecov, Coveralls)
pytest --cov=src --cov-report=xml

# Fail if coverage drops below threshold
pytest --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80

# Branch coverage
pytest --cov=src --cov-branch
```

Configure in `pyproject.toml`:

```toml
[tool.coverage.run]
source = ["src"]
omit = ["*/tests/*", "*/migrations/*", "*/__main__.py"]
branch = true

[tool.coverage.report]
fail_under = 80
show_missing = true
exclude_lines = [
    "pragma: no cover",
    "if TYPE_CHECKING:",
    "if __name__ == .__main__.",
    "raise NotImplementedError",
]
```

## Async Testing

Install pytest-asyncio for async/await support.

```bash
pip install pytest-asyncio
```

```python
import pytest
import httpx

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_async_fetch():
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
        resp = await client.get("https://api.example.com/health")
        assert resp.status_code == 200

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_async_db():
    async with get_async_session() as session:
        result = await session.execute(select(User))
        users = result.scalars().all()
        assert len(users) > 0
```

### Async Fixtures

```python
@pytest.fixture
async def async_client():
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(base_url="http://localhost:8000") as client:
        yield client

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_api_endpoint(async_client):
    resp = await async_client.get("/api/items")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
```

### Auto Mode

Set `asyncio_mode = "auto"` to skip the `@pytest.mark.asyncio` decorator — any async test function is detected automatically.

```toml
[tool.pytest.ini_options]
asyncio_mode = "auto"
```

## Parallel Execution

Use pytest-xdist to run tests across multiple CPUs.

```bash
pip install pytest-xdist

# Auto-detect CPU count
pytest -n auto

# Specific worker count
pytest -n 4

# Distribute by file (each worker gets whole files)
pytest -n auto --dist loadfile

# Distribute by group (tests in same class stay together)
pytest -n auto --dist loadgroup
```

Important: tests must be independent. Shared mutable state between tests will cause flaky failures under parallel execution. Use fixtures with proper scoping.

## Configuration

Configure pytest in `pyproject.toml` (preferred), `pytest.ini`, or `setup.cfg`.

```toml
[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
asyncio_mode = "auto"
addopts = "-v --tb=short --strict-markers -x"
markers = [
    "slow: marks tests as slow (deselect with '-m \"not slow\"')",
    "integration: integration tests requiring external services",
    "e2e: end-to-end tests",
]
filterwarnings = [
    "error",
    "ignore::DeprecationWarning",
]
log_cli = true
log_cli_level = "WARNING"
```

## CLI Reference

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `pytest` | Run all tests |
| `pytest -v` | Verbose output |
| `pytest -x` | Stop on first failure |
| `pytest -s` | Show print output (disable capture) |
| `pytest --tb=short` | Short tracebacks |
| `pytest --tb=no` | No tracebacks |
| `pytest -k "pattern"` | Filter by name pattern |
| `pytest -m "marker"` | Filter by marker expression |
| `pytest --co` | Collect only (show tests, don't run) |
| `pytest -n auto` | Parallel execution (xdist) |
| `pytest --cov=src` | Coverage report |
| `pytest --cov-fail-under=80` | Fail below threshold |
| `pytest --lf` | Rerun last failures only |
| `pytest --ff` | Run failures first |
| `pytest -p no:warnings` | Disable warnings plugin |
| `pytest --durations=10` | Show 10 slowest tests |
| `pytest --maxfail=3` | Stop after 3 failures |

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `PYTEST_CURRENT_TEST` | Set by pytest during test execution (test nodeid) |
| `PYTEST_ADDOPTS` | Extra command-line options appended to every run |
| `PYTEST_PLUGINS` | Comma-separated list of plugin modules to load |
| `CI` | Many plugins detect CI mode for adjusted behavior |
| `NO_COLOR` | Disable colored output when set |
| `PY_COLORS` | Set to `0` to disable or `1` to force colors |

## Plugin Ecosystem

| Plugin | Purpose | Install |
|--------|---------|---------|
| pytest-cov | Code coverage reporting | `pip install pytest-cov` |
| pytest-asyncio | Async/await test support | `pip install pytest-asyncio` |
| pytest-mock | `mocker` fixture (wraps unittest.mock) | `pip install pytest-mock` |
| pytest-xdist | Parallel and distributed testing | `pip install pytest-xdist` |
| pytest-randomly | Randomize test order | `pip install pytest-randomly` |
| pytest-timeout | Per-test timeout enforcement | `pip install pytest-timeout` |
| pytest-env | Set environment variables in config | `pip install pytest-env` |
| pytest-sugar | Better progress bar and output | `pip install pytest-sugar` |
| pytest-benchmark | Performance benchmarking | `pip install pytest-benchmark` |
| pytest-httpx | Mock httpx requests | `pip install pytest-httpx` |
| pytest-django | Django test integration | `pip install pytest-django` |
| pytest-factoryboy | Factory Boy integration for fixtures | `pip install pytest-factoryboy` |

## Project Layout

```
project/
  pyproject.toml            # pytest + coverage config
  src/
    myapp/
      __init__.py
      services.py
      models.py
  tests/
    conftest.py             # shared fixtures, hooks
    test_services.py        # unit tests for services
    test_models.py          # unit tests for models
    integration/
      conftest.py           # integration-specific fixtures
      test_api.py           # API integration tests
      test_database.py      # DB integration tests
```

sections.title: Quick start — install and first test
sections.body: Install pytest with `pip install pytest` (or `uv add pytest`, `poetry add --group dev pytest`). Create `tests/test_example.py` with functions prefixed `test_`. Use plain `assert` statements — pytest introspects them on failure to show detailed diffs. Run with `pytest` from the project root. Add `-v` for verbose output, `-x` to stop on first failure. Configure `testpaths = ["tests"]` in `pyproject.toml` under `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` to lock discovery to your test directory.

sections.title: Fixtures — scope, yield, conftest.py
sections.body: Fixtures provide dependency injection for tests. Decorate with `@pytest.fixture` and reference by parameter name. Use `yield` for teardown — code after yield runs even on failure. Scopes: `function` (default, per-test), `class`, `module`, `package`, `session`. Shared fixtures go in `conftest.py` — loaded automatically, no imports. Built-in fixtures: `tmp_path` (temp directory), `monkeypatch` (env/attr patching), `capsys` (capture stdout/stderr), `caplog` (capture logging), `request` (fixture metadata). Use `autouse=True` to apply a fixture to every test. Fixture factories return callables for parameterized creation. Parametrized fixtures (`params=[...]`) run dependent tests once per parameter value.

sections.title: Data-driven testing with parametrize
sections.body: Use `@pytest.mark.parametrize("arg1,arg2", [(val1, val2), ...])` to run one test with multiple inputs. Add `ids=[...]` for readable test names. Stack multiple `@parametrize` decorators for cross-product combinations. Embed marks in parameter sets with `pytest.param(val, marks=pytest.mark.xfail)`. Parametrized fixtures (`@pytest.fixture(params=[...])`) run all dependent tests per parameter. Use indirect parametrize (`indirect=True`) to pass values through a fixture before reaching the test.

sections.title: Skip, xfail, and custom markers
sections.body: Built-in markers: `@pytest.mark.skip(reason=...)` unconditionally skip, `@pytest.mark.skipif(condition, reason=...)` conditional skip, `@pytest.mark.xfail(reason=...)` expected failure. Custom markers label tests for selective running: define in `pyproject.toml` markers list, apply with `@pytest.mark.slow`, run with `pytest -m "not slow"`. Use `--strict-markers` to catch undefined markers. Marker expressions support `and`, `or`, `not`, and parentheses: `pytest -m "integration and not slow"`.

sections.title: Coverage with pytest-cov
sections.body: Install `pytest-cov` for coverage reporting. Run `pytest --cov=src --cov-report=term-missing` for a terminal report showing uncovered lines. Use `--cov-report=html` for an interactive HTML report in `htmlcov/`. Set `--cov-fail-under=80` to fail the build below a threshold. Enable branch coverage with `--cov-branch`. Configure in `pyproject.toml` under `[tool.coverage.run]` (source, omit, branch) and `[tool.coverage.report]` (fail_under, show_missing, exclude_lines). For CI, use `--cov-report=xml` to feed Codecov or Coveralls.

sections.title: Async testing with pytest-asyncio
sections.body: Install `pytest-asyncio` for async/await test support. Mark async tests with `@pytest.mark.asyncio` or set `asyncio_mode = "auto"` in config for automatic detection. Async fixtures use `async def` with `yield` for teardown. Combine with `httpx.AsyncClient` for testing async HTTP code and `AsyncMock` from `unittest.mock` for mocking async dependencies. Event loop scope defaults to function; use `@pytest.fixture(scope="session")` with `loop_scope="session"` for session-scoped async fixtures in pytest-asyncio 0.23+.

sections.title: Parallel execution with pytest-xdist
sections.body: Install `pytest-xdist` for parallel test execution. Run `pytest -n auto` to auto-detect CPU count, or `pytest -n 4` for a fixed worker count. Distribution modes: `loadscope` (group by module/class), `loadfile` (group by file), `loadgroup` (explicit grouping via `@pytest.mark.xdist_group`). Tests must be independent — no shared mutable state, no reliance on execution order. Session-scoped fixtures are instantiated once per worker, not once globally. For truly shared state across workers, use `tmp_path_factory` with file locks or a shared database.

sections.title: Essential plugin ecosystem
sections.body: Core plugins: `pytest-cov` (coverage), `pytest-asyncio` (async), `pytest-mock` (mocker fixture wrapping unittest.mock), `pytest-xdist` (parallel). Quality-of-life: `pytest-randomly` (randomize order to catch hidden dependencies), `pytest-timeout` (per-test timeouts), `pytest-sugar` (better progress output), `pytest-env` (set env vars in config). Domain-specific: `pytest-django` (Django), `pytest-httpx` (mock httpx), `pytest-factoryboy` (factory_boy integration), `pytest-benchmark` (perf benchmarks). Install from PyPI: `pip install pytest-cov pytest-asyncio pytest-mock pytest-xdist`. Check installed plugins: `pytest --co -q` or `pytest -p no:randomly` to disable one.

scripts.name: Run pytest with common options
scripts.description: Run pytest with configurable verbosity, stop-on-failure, coverage, parallel execution, and CI mode support. Activates virtualenv if found.
scripts.inline:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
TEST_PATH="${1:-tests}"
VERBOSE="${VERBOSE:-true}"
STOP_FIRST="${STOP_FIRST:-false}"
COVER="${COVER:-false}"
PARALLEL="${PARALLEL:-false}"
ARGS=("$TEST_PATH")
if [ "$VERBOSE" = "true" ]; then ARGS+=("-v" "--tb=short"); fi
if [ "$STOP_FIRST" = "true" ]; then ARGS+=("-x"); fi
if [ "$COVER" = "true" ]; then ARGS+=("--cov=src" "--cov-report=term-missing"); fi
if [ "$PARALLEL" = "true" ]; then ARGS+=("-n" "auto"); fi
pytest "${ARGS[@]}"

test_path.description: Path to test file or directory

duration_secs.description: Total test run duration in seconds

depends.reason: Tests should run in an isolated virtual environment
Install pytest and plugins via pip, uv, or poetry

author: dojo-community
